items: Rule Out Medical Causes: Schedule a vet appointment specifically requesting urinalysis and bloodwork for litter box avoidance — medical conditions like UTI, kidney disease, and diabetes must be ruled out before trying any behavioral fixes.

Rule Out Medical Causes: For male cats, report any straining to urinate, crying during urination, or producing only small amounts of urine immediately, as these are emergency signs of a potentially life-threatening blockage.
Rule Out Medical Causes: Request a urinalysis to check for infection, crystals, and blood in the urine, and blood work to evaluate kidney function and glucose levels at your vet visit.

Rule Out Medical Causes: Understand that a vet visit costing $100-$300 is far less expensive than replacing damaged flooring and is the only way to rule out the most dangerous medical causes of litter box avoidance.
Fix the Litter Box Setup: Apply the one box per cat plus one extra rule in your home, placing at least one box on each floor level so no cat has to travel far when they need to go.

Fix the Litter Box Setup: Replace any covered litter box with an uncovered one because covered boxes trap ammonia fumes and allow other cats to ambush the cat exiting, making the experience unpleasant.

Fix the Litter Box Setup: Upgrade to a box sized 1.5 times the length of your cat from nose to base of tail, using a storage tote with a cut-out entrance if a properly sized traditional box is unavailable.

Fix the Litter Box Setup: Fill the box with 2 inches of unscented clumping litter, scoop it daily, and perform a complete litter change weekly so the box never reaches a smell level that would deter your cat.

Reduce Stress and Enrich: Plug a Feliway diffuser into the room where your cat spends the most time, replacing the refill every 30 days and allowing a full 2-3 weeks before deciding whether it's helping.
Reduce Stress and Enrich: Add vertical space throughout your home with cat trees, shelves, or window perches positioned near windows so your cat feels safer elevated off the ground.

Reduce Stress and Enrich: Schedule 10-15 minutes of active play every day using whip-around toys, laser pointers, or feather wands to reduce general anxiety and boredom that can contribute to stress-related elimination.

Reduce Stress and Enrich: Provide at least one hiding spot in each room using cardboard boxes with blankets or covered pet carriers so your cat always has a fallback safe space when feeling threatened.
